title: Mole Negro Oaxacan Black Mole

seo.title: How to Make Mole Negro Oaxacan Black Mole — Easy Mexican Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Mole Negro Oaxacan Black Mole. This easy Mexican recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.

easy Mole Negro Oaxacan Black Mole recipe
authentic Mexican Mole Negro Oaxacan Black Mole
lowCompetitionKeywords: homemade Mole Negro Oaxacan Black Mole

seo.h1: Authentic Mole Negro Oaxacan Black Mole Recipe
h2Headings: What is Mole Negro Oaxacan Black Mole?

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Mexican Mole Negro Oaxacan Black Mole recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Mole Negro Oaxacan Black Mole — a beloved Mexican classic

description: Mole Negro Oaxacan Black Mole is a rich, complex, and deeply flavorful sauce, a true masterpiece of Mexican cuisine. This traditional dish from Oaxaca combines a multitude of ingredients including various chiles, spices, nuts, seeds, and chocolate, resulting in a dark, velvety sauce that is both sweet and savory. It's often served over poultry, making for an unforgettable and celebratory meal.

instructions.title: Prepare and Toast Chiles
instructions.text: Wipe dried chiles clean. Heat a dry comal or skillet over medium heat. Toast each chile for 30 seconds per side until fragrant, being careful not to burn them. Remove from heat, place in a bowl, cover with hot water, and let them rehydrate for 20-30 minutes until soft. Drain, reserving the soaking liquid.

instructions.title: Roast Aromatics and Fry Ingredients
instructions.text: Roast tomatoes, tomatillos, onion, and garlic on a comal or baking sheet until softened and slightly charred. In a separate pan with a little lard or oil, lightly fry the almonds, peanuts, sesame seeds, raisins, tortillas, bread, and plantain slices until golden. Set aside all ingredients.

instructions.title: Toast Spices and Grind
instructions.text: In the same pan, lightly toast the cinnamon stick, cloves, peppercorns, cumin seeds, and anise seeds until fragrant, about 1-2 minutes. Combine these toasted spices with the dried oregano and thyme. You can use a molcajete or spice grinder for this step.

instructions.title: Blend the Mole Paste
instructions.text: In batches, combine the rehydrated chiles, roasted vegetables, fried nuts, seeds, fruits, bread, plantain, and toasted spices in a blender. Add enough reserved chile soaking liquid and chicken broth to create a smooth, thick paste. Blend thoroughly until no large pieces remain. Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl, pressing down on solids to extract all liquid, discarding any remaining pulp.

instructions.title: Simmer the Mole Sauce
instructions.text: Heat 1/4 cup of lard or oil in a large, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Carefully pour the strained mole paste into the hot lard. It will splatter. Fry the mole, stirring constantly, for at least 20-30 minutes. This step is crucial for developing the deep flavor and color. Reduce heat to medium-low.

instructions.title: Finish and Season the Mole
instructions.text: Gradually stir in the remaining chicken broth, about 1/2 cup at a time, allowing it to incorporate fully before adding more. Add the chopped Mexican chocolate, sugar or piloncillo, and salt to taste. Continue to simmer the mole, stirring occasionally, for at least 1 hour, or up to 2 hours, until it thickens to your desired consistency and the flavors meld beautifully. Adjust seasoning as needed.

instructions.title: Serve with Chicken
instructions.text: Once the mole is ready, add the cooked chicken pieces directly into the sauce and simmer for another 10-15 minutes to allow the chicken to absorb the rich flavors. Serve hot, garnished with a sprinkle of sesame seeds, alongside warm corn tortillas and Mexican rice.

tips: Toasting the chiles properly is key; avoid burning them as this will make the mole bitter. A quick toast until fragrant is enough.
Do not skip the straining step. While tedious, it ensures a silky-smooth mole. For an even finer texture, strain twice.
Frying the mole paste in hot lard for a long time (sofrito) is essential for depth of flavor and color. Be patient and stir constantly to prevent sticking.
article: Mole Negro Oaxacan Black Mole is a beloved Mexican Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across Mexican. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
